posts.content:
Predictions: 6 of 11 Winners, 2 Perfect, 380 Points
|
Tied for 125th

Silva vs. Podlesniy
Josiel Silva, TKO, R4
Josiel top 3 chads in ACA

Outlaw vs. Ramos
Sidney Outlaw, DEC
Outlaw is gonna be unstoppable in ACA lol. He’s so boring but is an elite wrestler with great top control. Ramos is a great bjj player but he will resign to playing guard off of his back and getting smashed. Surprised to see so many on ko, outlaw does no damage with his top control

Kazaryan vs. Dzhibilov
Ayk Kazaryan, DEC
David is okay but I thought he got lucky to beat Valiev. Ayk is very underrated

Kakhramonov vs. Barsegyan
Saidyokub Kakhramonov, TKO, R1
Love seeing Saidyokub back in mma and staying active but damn, aca can’t get a quality opponent for him? This does nothing but gets him another finish. Saidyokub is an elite wrestler, he should maul most in ACA

Mishkevich vs. Froes
Vladislav Mishkevich, TKO, R1
Style Deadpool is not very good but Froes sadly doesn’t have it anymore

Abreu vs. Grishenko
Kirill Grishenko, TKO, R1
Abreu is a jobber

posts.author: elitemmapicks
posts.content:
Predictions: 6 of 12 Winners, 3 Perfect, 390 Points
|
Tied for 109th

Vladislav Mishkevich vs Felipe Froes
87% of Tapology users are picking Mishkevich but Froes opened at -105 on BetOnline and moved to -135
I like taking Froes via decision, which 73% of users picking Froes are taking

Sidney Outlaw vs Davi Ramos
52% of Tapology users picking Outlaw are picking KO as the method of victory
Outlaw has a 1:3 KO win/loss ratio, a 10:1 submission win/loss ratio, and a 10:2 decision win/loss ratio
Ramos has 2:1 KO win/loss ratio, a 7:0 submission win/loss ratio, and a 3:7 decision win/loss ratio
I like taking Outlaw via decision, which 42% of users picking Outlaw are taking

posts.author: AlbertEinstein
posts.content:
Predictions: 6 of 12 Winners, 5 Perfect, 440 Points
|
Tied for 47th with 1 other

Silva vs. Podlesniy
Josiel Silva, DEC
Eurasia's #14 P4P vs #92 P4P | Josiel pulls it off only slightly, split decison. I like Aleksandr's mix of ground and striking, plus the age and height advantage, but I don't see it being enough to take out Silva. Too many puzzles on the feet to take down Josiel and get a submission.

Abreu vs. Grishenko
Kirill Grishenko, DEC
Wrestler vs Striker | I like the competition Kirill has fought, I've seen tape on him, but boy does he not have a ton of fights. Grishenko got heel hooked by Buchecha, which does not seem to have any sort of striking game, that worries me for how Klidson could mix it up and get Kirill down. Otherwise, I think Grishenko has the cleaner record. 29-28 SD for Grishenko

Outlaw vs. Ramos
Sidney Outlaw, DEC
Eurasia's #21 P4P vs #253 P4P | I already like Sidney Outlaw's physical traits, younger and has a better reach than that of Davi Ramos. Outlaw should be able to stifle the ground game of Ramos, making this a back and forth fight leading to an Outlaw decision. Ramos can definitely get this done via knockout, It seems that sidney has a glass chin but Davi will certainly need to face adversity for it.

Dzhibilov vs. Kerimov
Rustam Kerimov, DEC
Eurasia's #216 P4P vs #16 P4P | I'm taking Rustam by decision, I trust in his expertise to tag David and point-fight. David Dzhibilov is very good, I just don't see the records being comparable at this time. 29-28 Kerimov

Mishkevich vs. Froes
Vladislav Mishkevich, TKO, R2
As much as I think Felipe Froes has a better chance than the odds may have it, I am going Vadislav Mishkevich by knockout. I highly rate Nova União, and Felipe has the slightly better record in my eyes. However, Froes just recently got knocked out in his last match, I very much see that a possibility again.

Kolodko vs. Pereira
Erivan Pereira, TKO, R1
Vicious knockout from Erivan Pereira - I do not like the inactivity from the older Ruslan Kolodko and Erivan has to be on the sauce. His body is more built than the Taj Mahal.

Erokhin vs. Starodub
Evgeny Erokhin, TKO, R1
This is a tough matchup! Evgeny is presumably much older - as we do not know - but is a KO machine, and we all know power stays with age. Sergey is very green in comparison but I like the well-rounded nature of his finishes. Sergey needs to take Erokhin down and remove as much of that power as possible, but I don't think he has the tools to do that to a vet without taking some heat.

Chmel vs. Imarov
Manas Imarov
I am going Manas Imarov by sub. There's levels to this and Sergey has not proven he can fight someone at this caliber thus far.

Kovalev vs. Mamedov
Kamal Mamedov, DEC
Despite Kamal being so green, I think Namedov will get this one done. Kovalev has lost at ACA often. I wouldn't put money on this one, but I like the randomness of young-blood. 29-28

Dolgov vs. Polaquini
Ewerton Polaquini, DEC
Eurasia's #191 P4P vs #363 P4P | At first glance, I thought Ewerton could get this done easilly with his knockout power. Despite Polaquini's recent record being questionable, losing to streaking fighters only elevates his position. Looking deeper, Dolgov may have a weaker chin due to the very recent KO, but his experience in the promotion proves he has the grit to fight, which is harder to say for the KO-guy getting a freak submission. Im gonna hedge my bets that Dolgov uses his movement to avoid the single KO-shot. 29-28?

posts.author: AlbertEinstein
posts.content:
Predictions: 6 of 12 Winners, 5 Perfect, 440 Points
|
Tied for 47th with 1 other

Zasinets vs. Silva
Michel Silva, TKO, R2
Dmitry is quite green as opposed to his opponent Michel Silva. Michel may be older, but he is also taller, has longer reach and has a good amount of experience in this promotion to begin with. Looking at prior opponents, there is a clear discrepancy between skilled competition leaning the way of Silva.

Tsiptauri vs. Barseghyan
Mkhitar Barseghyan, DEC
Vazha the bantamweight vs Mkhitar the flyweight - despite the physical size advantage Tsiptauri will have, I like the activity of Barseghyan more. Movement and avoiding the takedown will be paramount to keeping the scorecards going Mkhitar's way. Both fighters have lost to comparable good competition,  I go with the guy's natural weight class.
